## 3. Major Financial AI Company News

- Date: 2026-03-27
  Link: https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/chinas-moonshot-ai-seeks-listing-in-hong-kong-under-heightened-scrutiny-7a0a71ef
  Summary: Moonshot AI is considering a Hong Kong IPO restructuring while raising fresh capital at roughly an $18 billion valuation.
  Why it matters: Capital remains available for top-tier Chinese labs despite tighter scrutiny of offshore structures; this is a signal on investor appetite and China AI market durability.

## 4. Major AI Data Center News

- Date: 2026-03-28
  Link: https://www.wsj.com/tech/meta-agrees-to-fund-local-energy-infrastructure-for-louisiana-data-center-b795bc96
  Summary: Meta agreed to fund major power infrastructure for its Richland Parish, Louisiana AI campus, including gas generation, transmission, and storage.
  Why it matters: Power access is now a first-order competitive constraint in AI. Hyperscalers are moving upstream into energy and grid build-out to secure model-training capacity.

## 7. AI Workflows & Tactics

- Date: 2026-03-29
  Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DvNKYftvPW0
  Summary: The actionable workflow is not a consumer prompt trick; it is a product strategy. Take a strong base model, add proprietary last-mile interaction data, then post-train with curated reward/eval data for a narrow task domain.
  Why it matters: This is a practical path for product teams to reach frontier-like task performance without building a frontier base model.

  What it does: Produces task-specialized systems that outperform generic models in defined workflows such as support or coding assistance.

  Tools/models used: Open-weight or commercial base model, proprietary interaction logs, supervised fine-tuning pipeline, RL or preference optimization, internal eval set.

  Setup:
  1. Pick a narrow workflow with high interaction volume and measurable quality.
  2. Collect production traces, edits, outcomes, and human quality signals.
  3. Build a task-specific eval set from real failures and high-value edge cases.
  4. Fine-tune the base model on successful interaction data.
  5. Apply preference optimization or RL on curated quality data.
  6. Re-run against the internal eval set and iterate on failure clusters.
  7. Deploy behind product instrumentation so new usage becomes new training data.

  Available now: Yes, in principle. The gating factor is proprietary data quality, not model access.

## 9. Podcast Highlights

- Date: 2026-03-29
  Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DvNKYftvPW0
  Summary: The podcast argues the field is shifting from a single scaling-driven frontier toward “vertical” models optimized with proprietary interaction data and post-training for specific workflows.
  Why it matters: This reframes competitive advantage away from raw base-model access and toward ownership of workflow data, evals, and deployment loops.

  Topic: Vertical AI models
  What it is: Domain-specific systems built by taking a base model and pushing performance with last-mile product data and targeted post-training.
  Available now: Yes.
  How to use it:
  1. Identify a workflow where users repeatedly correct or rank model outputs.
  2. Capture those interactions as training data.
  3. Turn the best and worst outcomes into evals and preference data.
  4. Fine-tune and test against workflow-specific benchmarks before rollout.
  5. Keep the product-data-feedback loop closed so deployment improves the model.
  Direct reference: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DvNKYftvPW0

  Topic: Erosion of API moats
  What it is: If narrow-task performance can be won through post-training and proprietary data, model buyers have more incentive to customize open or lower-cost models in-house.
  Available now: Yes, as an operating model.
  How to use it:
  1. Audit where your current API spend maps to repetitive, high-volume tasks.
  2. Test whether a fine-tuned smaller/open model meets quality thresholds on internal evals.
  3. Keep premium frontier APIs only where generality or reasoning breadth still matters.
  Direct reference: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DvNKYftvPW0

  Topic: Model speciation
  What it is: Instead of one best general model, expect many high-performing specialist models tuned for coding, support, research, or other verticals.
  Available now: Emerging now.
  How to use it:
  1. Stop evaluating models only on generic public benchmarks.
  2. Build domain evals tied to business outcomes.
  3. Route workloads to specialized models where quality/cost is superior.
  Direct reference: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DvNKYftvPW0
